original_assessment.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
The control can be fully verified using Azure CLI commands and ARM APIs. The audit section details commands to retrieve App Service plan and its SKU information, making this control suitable for automated checking.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_plan_sku_supports_private_endpoints

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would perform the following checks:

1. **Azure Resource Enumeration:**
   - Enumerate Azure subscriptions and resource groups to identify App Services.
   - Use the following Azure CLI command to list all App Services:
     ```bash
     az webapp list --query "[].{name:name, resourceGroup:resourceGroup}"
     ```

2. **Retrieve App Service Plan Information:**
   - For each App Service, retrieve its corresponding App Service plan ID. This can be done using:
     ```bash
     az webapp show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> --query appServicePlanId
     ```

3. **Check SKU Tier and Name:**
   - For each App Service plan, retrieve its SKU tier and name with the following command:
     ```bash
     az appservice plan show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-service-plan-name> --query "{tier:sku.tier,name:sku.name}"
     ```

4. **Validation Criteria:**
   - Determine if the tier is one of the following approved options: **Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption**.
   - Confirm if the plan name matches one of the valid names listed: **B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1**.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - The audit will pass if all App Service plans support private endpoints based on the SKU tier and name. It will fail if any App Service plan does not meet the defined criteria for either tier or name.

6. **Example CLI Command for Validation:**
   - Use the commands mentioned above to output the current configurations and match them against the expected values for automated compliance validation.

review.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Classification:** The classification of the audit approach as Automated is appropriate. The use of Azure CLI commands and ARM APIs is a valid method for verifying the App Service plan SKU and its support for private endpoints.
- **Feasibility:** This check can indeed be performed using Azure APIs and CLI, as the necessary information about App Service plans and their SKUs can be retrieved programmatically.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- **Automation Details:** The automation details provided are mostly accurate. The CLI commands listed are correct for retrieving App Service and App Service plan information.
- **Program Name:** The program name `azure_app_service_plan_sku_supports_private_endpoints` follows the convention and clearly indicates the intent.
- **Completeness:** While the steps are technically correct, there is a minor oversight in not explicitly verifying the support for private endpoints directly. The SKU tier and name imply support, but a direct check would be more robust.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Direct Private Endpoint Support Check:** Consider including a direct check for private endpoint support, as some SKUs might have additional configurations or limitations that are not solely dependent on the SKU tier and name.
- **Error Handling:** Implement error handling in the automation script to manage scenarios where the App Service plan information retrieval fails or returns unexpected results.
- **Documentation:** Provide documentation or comments within the automation script to explain the logic behind the SKU tier and name validation criteria.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The approach and technical details are well-founded, and the use of Azure CLI and APIs is appropriate for this compliance check.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modification Needed:** Enhance the automation script to include a direct verification step for private endpoint support, beyond just checking the SKU tier and name. This could involve checking specific properties or configurations related to private endpoints within the App Service plan settings.
- **Justification:** While the current approach is mostly accurate, ensuring direct verification of private endpoint support will increase the robustness and reliability of the compliance check.

input_row.title: Ensure App Service plan SKU supports private endpoints
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. In the Properties pane, under Hosting, next to SKU and size, ensure that the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and that the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. 4. Repeat steps 1-3 for each app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to get the App Service plan ID: az webapp show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> -- query appServicePlanId For each App Service plan, run the following command to get the plan SKU tier and name: az appservice plan show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app- service-plan-name> --query "{tier:sku.tier,name:sku.name}" Ensure that the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and that the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list apps: Get-AzWebApp Run the following command to get the app in a resource group with a given name: $app = Get-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName <resource-group-name> -Name <app-name> Run the following command to get the App Service plan ID: $app.ServerFarmId Run the following command to get the App Service plan in a resource group with a given name: $plan = Get-AzAppServicePlan -ResourceGroupname <resource-group-name> -Name <app-service-plan-name> Run the following command to get the plan SKU tier and name: $plan.Sku | select-object Tier, Name Ensure that the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and that the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. Repeat for each app. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 546fe8d2-368d-4029-a418-6af48a7f61e5 - Name: 'App Service apps should use a SKU that supports private link'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. In the Properties pane, under Hosting, next to Name, click the App Service plan name. 4. Under Current App Service plan, next to Name, click the App Service plan name. 5. Under Essentials, next to Pricing plan, click the pricing plan name. 6. Select a pricing plan where the plan tier is one of the following: Basic, Standard, ElasticPremium, Premium, PremiumV2, Premium0V3, PremiumV3, PremiumMV3, IsolatedV2, IsolatedMV2, WorkflowStandard, FlexConsumption, and the plan name is one of the following: B1, B2, B3, S1, S2, S3, EP1, EP2, EP3, P1, P2, P3, P1V2, P2V2, P3V2, P0V3, P1V3, P2V3, P3V3, P1MV3, P2MV3, P3MV3, P4MV3, P5MV3, I1V2, I2V2, I3V2, I4V2, I5V2, I6V2, I1MV2, I2MV2, I3MV2, I4MV2, I5MV2, WS1, WS2, WS3, FC1. 7. Click Select. 8. Click Downgrade or Upgrade to confirm the change. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and App Service plan requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each App Service plan requiring remediation, run the following command to update the SKU: az appservice plan update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app- service-plan-name> --sku <sku> Default Value: The App Service plan is selected during creation. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-hosting-plans 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-private-endpoint 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/appservice/plan 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get-azwebapp 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get- azappserviceplan 7. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/app-service/linux/ 8. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/app-service/windows/ CIS Controls:
